articles.title: Hong Kong Plans New Rules for Ride-Hailing Apps Like Uber
articles.summary: The Hong Kong government is getting ready to create new laws that will control ride-hailing services, such as apps that let people book private cars online. Officials say they hope to start giving licences to ride-hailing platforms as early as November 2026, with driver and car licences available in the fourth quarter of this year. Right now, these services operate without a specific licence, which has caused arguments about fairness with traditional taxi drivers. The new rules aim to make the system safer and fairer for both passengers and drivers. This is a big change for how people travel around the city.

lv1: What kind of services is the Hong Kong government planning to make new rules for? (香港政府計劃為哪類服務制定新規則？)
When does the government hope to start giving licences to ride-hailing platforms? (政府希望何時開始向網約車平台發牌？)
lv2: Why do you think traditional taxi drivers might be unhappy about ride-hailing apps like Uber? (你認為傳統的士司機為何可能對網約車應用程式感到不滿？)
How might having a licence system make ride-hailing services safer for passengers? (發牌制度如何令乘客使用網約車服務更安全？)
lv3: Should the government treat ride-hailing apps and traditional taxis under the same rules, or should there be different systems for each? What are the benefits and drawbacks of each approach? (政府應否以相同規則規管網約車應用程式和傳統的士，還是各設不同制度？兩種做法各有什麼利弊？)

Technology is changing the way cities move people around. Do you think ride-hailing apps are overall good or bad for a city like Hong Kong? Consider the environment, employment, and passenger safety. (科技正在改變城市的交通方式。你認為網約車應用程式整體上對香港這類城市是好是壞？請從環境、就業及乘客安全三方面考慮。)

articles.title: Scientists Warn: Rivers Around the World Are Losing Oxygen Fast
articles.summary: Researchers have issued a serious warning that rivers all over the world are gradually running out of oxygen, and they say climate change is the biggest reason why. A large-scale study looked at more than 21,000 river systems across the globe and found that nearly four out of every five rivers are affected. When rivers lose oxygen, fish and other water creatures struggle to survive, and the whole underwater ecosystem can be damaged. Warmer temperatures caused by climate change make it harder for rivers to hold oxygen. Scientists hope this finding will push governments to take stronger action on climate change and protect freshwater environments for the future.

lv1: What is happening to rivers around the world according to scientists? (根據科學家的說法，全球河流正在發生什麼事？)
How many river systems did scientists study to find this out? (科學家研究了多少個河流系統來得出這個發現？)
lv2: Why does warmer weather caused by climate change make it harder for rivers to have enough oxygen? (氣候變化導致的天氣變暖，為何令河流更難維持足夠的氧氣量？)
What could happen to animals and plants living in rivers if the oxygen levels keep dropping? (如果河流氧氣水平持續下降，生活在河中的動植物會有什麼後果？)
lv3: If rivers lose oxygen and fish populations collapse, this could affect the food supply for millions of people. Who do you think should be responsible for solving this problem — individual people, companies, or governments? Explain your reasoning. (如果河流失去氧氣、魚類數量崩潰，可能影響數百萬人的糧食供應。你認為誰應負責解決這個問題——個人、企業還是政府？請解釋你的理由。)

Some people say economic growth is more important than protecting the environment. Others disagree. Using the example of rivers losing oxygen, argue either for or against prioritising the environment over economic development. (有人認為經濟增長比保護環境更重要，亦有人持相反意見。請以河流失氧為例，就「環境優先於經濟發展」的立場，提出支持或反對的論點。)

articles.title: 港府擬將橋嘴洲珊瑚區劃為海岸公園　環保團體憂範圍太小
articles.summary: 政府建議將西貢橋嘴洲附近的東西珊瑚區劃為新的海岸公園，為當地豐富的珊瑚生態提供法律保護。不過，環保團體批評有關建議屬「縮水」方案，指計劃未有將橋嘴洲南北兩個珊瑚群落納入保護範圍，是「斬件式」做法。他們促請政府重新檢視方案，擴大保護範圍，以確保整個珊瑚生態系統得到全面保護。珊瑚礁是海洋生物的重要棲息地，一旦受損將難以復原。

lv1: 政府建議將哪個地方的珊瑚區劃為海岸公園？
環保團體對政府的建議有什麼意見？
lv2: 為什麼環保團體認為只保護部分珊瑚區是不足夠的？
珊瑚礁對海洋生態有什麼重要性？如果珊瑚礁受到破壞，會帶來什麼影響？
lv3: 發展旅遊業與保護自然環境有時會產生矛盾。你認為政府在劃定海岸公園範圍時，應如何平衡生態保護與市民的休閒需要？
環保團體與政府對保護範圍有不同看法。你認為在制定環保政策時，政府應如何聽取不同持份者的意見，才算是負責任的決策方式？

articles.title: 食環署擬將新街市整體外判　解決空置攤檔問題
articles.summary: 為應對本港公眾街市長期出現大量空置攤檔的問題，食物環境衞生署建議在新落成的街市引入「整體承租」模式，即將整個街市交由一間私人公司管理和營運。當局認為此舉可提升街市的整體管理效率，吸引更多顧客光顧，並改善街市的衛生環境。不過，有意見擔心外判後租金可能上升，令小商販難以負擔，影響街坊日常購買新鮮食材的選擇和費用。

lv1: 食環署建議用什麼新方法管理香港的公眾街市？
現時香港街市面臨什麼主要問題？
lv2: 「整體承租」模式與現時的街市管理方式有何不同？這樣做有什麼好處？
為什麼有人擔心街市外判後，小商販和街坊會受到影響？
lv3: 公眾街市是香港社區文化的重要部分。你認為將街市外判給私人公司管理，對社區文化和基層市民的生活會帶來什麼正面或負面的影響？
政府在提供公共服務時，應否優先考慮效率還是可負擔性？以街市外判為例，分析兩者之間的取捨。
